response.justification: Bogdan is a technically accomplished individual contributor with 20 years of broad engineering experience and genuine developer tooling work — test frameworks, CI/CD infrastructure, and MCP code generators — but the resume contains zero evidence of people management: no direct reports, no hiring, no performance reviews, and no org-level leadership. The role is fundamentally a people management position with explicit minimums of 2 years managing teams, and the entire responsibilities section centers on 1:1s, performance expectations, and team development. Strong IC profile, wrong role.
strengths: Extensive developer tooling background including test frameworks (Fluent-Asserts, Trial), CI/CD infrastructure setup, and AI-agent/MCP tooling at re:cinq
Long, diverse career spanning 20 years across web, mobile, embedded, and high-throughput distributed systems indicating genuine technical breadth
Active knowledge-sharing through workshops, conference talks, and organizing Refactoring Dojos — signals coaching instincts relevant to an eventual management track
concerns: No people management experience anywhere in the resume — no direct reports, no hiring ownership, no performance or compensation conversations mentioned
No evidence of leading organizational change, setting team-level strategy, or owning a multi-quarter roadmap beyond solo/founder-mode open source work
Career trajectory is firmly on the IC path (Software Developer → Senior → Staff) with no signals of transition toward engineering management
